passages.title: How Crayons Are Made
passages.subject: ELA
passages.text:
Have you ever wondered how a crayon gets its bright color and smooth shape? Many factory-made crayons begin with paraffin wax and powdered pigment. At a factory, machines and workers turn these materials into the crayons you use. Crayons look simple, but making them takes care, heat, and a plan. The trip from wax to crayon happens in just a few steps, and each step has a job to do.

Melting the Wax

The first step is getting the wax ready. Crayons are made mostly of paraffin wax. Workers heat or keep the wax warm in large metal tanks until it is a clear liquid. In some factories, the wax even arrives in liquid form and must stay warm so it does not harden. A solid turns into a liquid when it gets hot enough, the way an ice cube melts in the warm sun. The wax is kept hot so it stays liquid until it is needed. The wax must be fully melted before any color can be added. Once the wax is fully liquid, it is smooth and runny and ready to take on color.

Adding the Color

Next comes the color. A worker blends in powdered pigment, the material that gives each crayon its shade. Workers add a measured amount of pigment; the amount depends on the shade. Some colors need more pigment and some need less to come out just right. Red powder makes red crayons, and blue powder makes blue. Factories may use one pigment or blend several. The wax is mixed until the pigment spreads evenly, so that no part of the batch turns out lighter or darker than the rest. Getting the color even is important, because every crayon from one batch should look the same. A single factory can make dozens of different shades this way, from bright red to deep purple.

Filling the Molds

The colored wax then flows into a mold. A mold is a flat metal tray full of crayon-shaped holes. The hot wax is poured carefully so that each hole fills all the way to the top. Cool water runs through pipes around the mold to help the wax harden quickly. As the liquid wax cools, it becomes solid again and keeps the shape of the mold. Because hundreds of holes are filled at the same time, hundreds of crayons can form all at once. The wax usually becomes solid in about four to seven minutes. Cooling the wax quickly lets the factory make many more crayons in a day.

Pushing Out and Checking

When the crayons are firm, a machine gently pushes them up and out of the mold. Now the factory must make sure each crayon is good enough to sell. Inspectors examine the crayons for breaks, chips, and bubbles. A crayon may have bubbles if the color was not mixed all the way. Any crayon with these problems is pulled out of the line. The rejected crayons are not thrown away, though — they are melted down and recast in a brand-new batch. Because the bad crayons are removed at this step, every crayon that keeps moving forward is smooth and whole.

Wrapping and Packing

The last steps are wrapping and packing. A fast machine wraps a paper label around the middle of each crayon. The label prints the color's name, so you can find the shade you want. Many crayons get a double layer of paper, which makes them stronger so they do not snap as easily. After that, the finished crayons are sorted by color and dropped into boxes. Some boxes hold only a few colors, while others hold every color of the rainbow, with each crayon made the very same way. Once the boxes are full, they are closed up and stacked for shipping. From there, the boxes are sent out for distribution.

So the next time you open a fresh box, remember the journey each crayon took — from melted wax and color powder, to a pool of bright color, to the smooth stick in your hand. A lot of careful steps, and a few very hot machines, go into a tool small enough to fit in your pocket. Each crayon may be small, but many people and machines helped make it.

textFeaturesJson.title: How a Crayon Is Made
textFeaturesJson.altText: Diagram: the five steps of making a crayon, in order — melt the wax, add the color, fill the mold, push out and check, wrap and pack.
textFeaturesJson.longDescription: This diagram shows 5 steps in order. Step 1: Melt the wax. Paraffin wax is heated or kept warm until it is liquid. Step 2: Add the color. Powdered pigment is blended in to give the wax its color. Step 3: Fill the mold. The colored wax is poured into crayon-shaped holes and cooled. Step 4: Push out and check. Hardened crayons are pushed out; broken or chipped ones are removed. Step 5: Wrap and pack. Each crayon gets a paper label, then crayons are sorted and boxed.

stages.label: Melt the wax
stages.caption: Paraffin wax is heated or kept warm until it is liquid.

stages.label: Add the color
stages.caption: Powdered pigment is blended in to give the wax its color.

stages.label: Fill the mold
stages.caption: The colored wax is poured into crayon-shaped holes and cooled.

stages.label: Push out and check
stages.caption: Hardened crayons are pushed out; broken or chipped ones are removed.

stages.label: Wrap and pack
stages.caption: Each crayon gets a paper label, then crayons are sorted and boxed.

factCheckNotesJson.claim: Crayons are made primarily of paraffin wax and color pigment.
factCheckNotesJson.sourceTitle: What is the Crayola Crayons manufacturing process and basic ingredients?

factCheckNotesJson.claim: The process melts paraffin wax and blends it with color pigments.
factCheckNotesJson.sourceTitle: What is the Crayola Crayons manufacturing process and basic ingredients?

factCheckNotesJson.claim: The wax is mixed with pre-measured amounts of color pigment; factories use individual pigments or mix several to make many colors.
factCheckNotesJson.sourceTitle: Can you provide information about the science of Crayola Crayons?

factCheckNotesJson.claim: The mixture is poured into molds where it solidifies in about four to seven minutes.
factCheckNotesJson.sourceTitle: What is the Crayola Crayons manufacturing process and basic ingredients?

factCheckNotesJson.claim: Pigments are added in powdered form because paraffin will not mix with water.
Paraffin is delivered warm/liquid and kept warm so it does not harden before use.
After cooling, crayons are pushed from the molds and inspected for breaks, chips, and bubbles (bubbles form from incomplete mixing).
Rejected crayons are returned for remelting and recasting.
Passing crayons are wrapped with paper labels (often a double wrap for strength), then filled into boxes for distribution.

items.subject: ELA
items.stem: Explain how wax becomes a wrapped crayon. Use at least two details from the passage to support your answer.
items.instructionText: Write a short answer that explains the ordered process and uses details from the passage.
items.expectedAnswerCore: Wax is melted, pigment is mixed in, colored wax is poured into molds and cooled, the crayons are checked, and then they are wrapped and boxed.

acceptableTextSupport.quotedSpan: The wax must be fully melted before any color can be added.
acceptableTextSupport.connectsToExpectedAnswer: Shows the first step before coloring.

acceptableTextSupport.quotedSpan: A worker blends in powdered pigment
acceptableTextSupport.connectsToExpectedAnswer: Shows how the wax gets its color.

acceptableTextSupport.quotedSpan: The hot wax is poured carefully so that each hole fills all the way to the top.
acceptableTextSupport.connectsToExpectedAnswer: Shows how the crayon shape is formed.

acceptableTextSupport.quotedSpan: Inspectors examine the crayons for breaks, chips, and bubbles.
acceptableTextSupport.connectsToExpectedAnswer: Shows the checking step before wrapping.

acceptableTextSupport.quotedSpan: A fast machine wraps a paper label around the middle of each crayon.

commonIncompletePatterns: Names only one step without explaining order.
Copies a sentence but does not explain how the process moves from wax to crayon.
Skips the checking or wrapping stage.

rubric.points3: Explains the ordered crayon-making process with at least two accurate passage details and clear reasoning about how the steps connect.
rubric.points2: Explains part of the sequence with at least one accurate detail, or gives two details with limited explanation.
rubric.points1: Gives one relevant detail or a vague sequence with little explanation.
rubric.points0: Gives an incorrect, unsupported, or off-topic response.

scoreBandExamples.response: First the wax is melted, then pigment is mixed in so it has color. The liquid wax is poured into molds and cooled, then workers check it before a machine wraps the crayons and boxes them.
scoreBandExamples.why: Explains the sequence and uses multiple accurate details.

scoreBandExamples.response: The wax melts and gets color. Then it goes in molds and is wrapped.
scoreBandExamples.why: Mostly correct sequence but limited support and explanation.

scoreBandExamples.response: Crayons are made with wax and paper.
scoreBandExamples.why: Mentions relevant materials but does not explain the process.

responseSpecJson.stem: Explain how wax becomes a wrapped crayon. Use at least two details from the passage to support your answer.
responseSpecJson.instructionText: Write a short answer that explains the ordered process and uses details from the passage.

plausibleWrongRationales.rainbow_boxes: That statement happens after wrapping and sorting.

rows.label: 5 — Wrap and pack
rows.rationale: The packing stage is when crayons are sorted and placed into boxes.

plausibleWrongRationales.wax_fully_melted: That statement belongs to the first stage.
plausibleWrongRationales.hundreds_form: That statement belongs to filling the mold.

columns.label: The wax must be fully melted before any color can be added.
Hundreds of crayons form at the same time.
Some boxes hold every color of the rainbow.

items.subject: ELA
items.stem: Match each word from the passage to its meaning.
items.instructionText: Choose the best match for each row.

rows.label: evenly
rows.rationale: The pigment spreads evenly when no part is lighter or darker.

plausibleWrongRationales.melted_and_shaped_again: Recast, not evenly, means shaped again.

rows.label: runny
rows.rationale: The liquid wax is smooth and runny when it is ready to flow.

plausibleWrongRationales.melted_and_shaped_again: Recast means made again after melting.

rows.label: recast
rows.rationale: Rejected crayons are melted down and recast in a new batch.
